claims.claim: Zero-knowledge proofs will transform the web3 stack
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Web3 creator economy (music, media, gaming, NFTs)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Centralized, custodial intermediaries masquerading as crypto
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Risk-on: lean in hard, build through the downturn
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Active governance opposition to deploying Uniswap on BNB Chain via Wormhole — a specific anti-bridge/security stance not anticipated
New enthusiasm for NEAR as an infrastructure chain (where prediction had named Solana)
Crypto-meets-web2 payments rails (Loop + Stripe integration) as a positive theme
Articulated a 'value-capture paradox' / network-effects winner-take-all crypto business-model thesis
Broader cultural/political commentary: hostility toward builders and critique of top-down government science funding in favor of bottom-up market mechanisms
notes: Window is Jan–Jun 2023. Most core technology theses (ZK, privacy via Iron Fish mainnet, AI x crypto via Gensyn + 'collective AI', DAOs via prtyDAO, multi-chain via LayerZero, ETH, risk-on via Alongside/Gensyn leads and hiring) are clearly confirmed. The regressive-regulation concern is confirmed at stance level via posts on hostility to innovation and status-quo entrenched interests. DePIN/Helium, creator economy (music/NFTs/gaming), wallet UX, centralized-intermediary contagion, and Solana specifically were not addressed in the window — marked absent, not contradicted. No stances were reversed.
